data.content:
### Specify Multiple Allowed Tools (Comma-Separated) in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This example demonstrates how to grant a slash command access to multiple tools using a comma-separated string in the `allowed-tools` field. This method allows for specifying several distinct tools required for the command's execution. Tool access is crucial for security and ensuring the command operates within defined boundaries.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit
```

--------------------------------

### Define Basic YAML Frontmatter Structure for Slash Commands

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This snippet illustrates the basic structure of YAML frontmatter used to configure slash commands. It includes common fields such as `description`, `allowed-tools`, `model`, and `argument-hint` to define metadata before the command's prompt content. All frontmatter fields are optional.

```markdown
---
description: Brief description
allowed-tools: Read, Write
model: sonnet
argument-hint: [arg1] [arg2]
---

Command prompt content here...
```

--------------------------------

### Allow All Tools in YAML Frontmatter (Not Recommended)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example illustrates how to grant a slash command access to all available tools using the wildcard `*`. While providing maximum flexibility, this configuration is generally not recommended due to significant security implications, as it removes all tool-based restrictions. It should be used with extreme caution.

```yaml
allowed-tools: "*"
```

--------------------------------

### Restrict Command to Read-Only Operations in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML configuration restricts a slash command to perform only read-only operations using `Read, Grep`. By explicitly limiting tool access, this ensures the command cannot modify any data or system state, significantly enhancing security. This is a crucial practice for commands intended for inspection or information retrieval.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Read, Grep  # Read-only command
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Single Allowed Tool in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML snippet illustrates how to define a single tool that a slash command is permitted to use. The `allowed-tools` field restricts or specifies tool access, enhancing security and clarity by explicitly listing required capabilities. If omitted, tool permissions are inherited from the conversation.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Read
```

--------------------------------

### Minimal SlashCommand - No Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Demonstrates the simplest SlashCommand format with only the command body and no frontmatter configuration. This minimal approach works when no special configuration is needed beyond the command description.

```markdown
Review this code for common issues and suggest improvements.
```

--------------------------------

### Frontmatter Fields Reference - YAML Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/README.md

Quick reference table of common YAML frontmatter fields used in slash commands. Each field controls specific aspects of command behavior including tool access restrictions, model selection, argument documentation, and execution mode. These fields enable fine-grained control over how commands interact with tools and models.

```yaml
description: "Review code for issues"
allowed-tools: "Read, Bash(git:*)"
model: "sonnet"
argument-hint: "[pr-number] [priority]"
disable-model-invocation: true
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Slash Command File Format - Markdown with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/README.md

Demonstrates the fundamental structure of a Claude Code slash command file. Commands use Markdown format with optional YAML frontmatter for configuration. The frontmatter defines metadata like description, allowed tools, and argument hints, followed by the command prompt content that can include dynamic arguments, file references, and bash execution.

```markdown
---
description: Brief description
argument-hint: [arg1] [arg2]
allowed-tools: Read, Bash(git:*)
---

Command prompt content with:
- Arguments: $1, $2, or $ARGUMENTS
- Files: @path/to/file
- Bash: !`command here`
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Claude Model in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

These YAML examples illustrate how to specify which Claude model (`haiku`, `sonnet`, `opus`) should execute a slash command. This field allows tailoring the model's capability to the command's complexity, optimizing for speed, cost, or reasoning power. If not specified, the model inherits from the conversation context.

```yaml
model: haiku    # Fast, efficient for simple tasks
```

```yaml
model: sonnet   # Balanced performance (default)
```

```yaml
model: opus     # Maximum capability for complex tasks
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Multiple Allowed Tools (Array) in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML snippet shows how to define multiple allowed tools for a slash command using a list (array) format. This approach provides a clear and structured way to specify various tools, including specialized ones like `Bash(git:*)`, enhancing readability and maintainability of the frontmatter configuration. It's an alternative to the comma-separated string format.

```yaml
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash(git:*)
```

--------------------------------

### Simple SlashCommand with Description

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand with only description in frontmatter. Shows the basic configuration level including YAML frontmatter delimiters and command body without additional properties.

```markdown
---
description: Review code for issues
---

Review this code for common issues and suggest improvements.
```

--------------------------------

### SlashCommand with Single Argument Hint

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Complete SlashCommand example with frontmatter and body demonstrating argument-hint usage for a simple command. Shows how argument-hint integrates with description and command body using positional parameter references ($1).

```markdown
---
description: Fix issue by number
argument-hint: [issue-number]
---

Fix issue #$1...
```

--------------------------------

### Manual-Only Command with disable-model-invocation

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand configured for manual-only execution with disable-model-invocation set to true. Example demonstrates preventing automated invocation for commands requiring user judgment, such as production deployment approvals.

```markdown
---
description: Approve deployment to production
disable-model-invocation: true
---
```

--------------------------------

### Standard SlashCommand with Description and Tools

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand configuration including description and allowed-tools properties. Demonstrates specifying tool access with command filters, including embedded command execution using backticks and exclamation marks.

```markdown
---
description: Review Git changes
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*), Read
---

Current changes: !`git diff --name-only`

Review each changed file for:
- Code quality
- Potential bugs
- Best practices
```

--------------------------------

### Define Command with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-structure/SKILL.md

Markdown file format for command definitions with YAML frontmatter containing name and description. Implementation instructions follow the frontmatter delimiter. Auto-discovered from commands/ directory and integrated as native slash commands.

```markdown
---
name: command-name
description: Command description
---

Command implementation instructions...
```

--------------------------------

### Filter Bash Commands by Pattern in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

These YAML snippets demonstrate how to restrict Bash tool access to specific command patterns using filters like `git:*`, `npm:*`, or `docker:*`. This granular control enhances security by preventing arbitrary Bash command execution, allowing only predefined operations related to specific tools. It ensures commands can only perform actions relevant to their purpose.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*)           # Only git commands
```

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(npm:*)           # Only npm commands
```

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(docker:*)        # Only docker commands
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command Description in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

These YAML examples demonstrate how to set the `description` field in command frontmatter. The description is a string that briefly explains the command's purpose and is displayed in `/help` output. It's recommended to keep it under ~60 characters and start with a verb.

```yaml
description: Review code for security issues
```

```yaml
description: Deploy to staging environment
```

```yaml
description: Generate API documentation
```

--------------------------------

### SlashCommand with Options and Argument Hints

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand configuration showing argument-hint with options parameter. Demonstrates how to document commands that accept both required arguments and optional configuration parameters.

```markdown
---
description: Run tests with options
argument-hint: [test-pattern] [options]
---

Run tests matching $1 with options: $2
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Specific Bash Command Output in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example shows how to enable a command to execute and retrieve output from specific Bash commands, like `git status:*` and `git diff:*`. This fine-grained control allows for specific shell interactions while maintaining security by limiting the scope of executable Bash commands. It's useful for commands needing to report on specific system states.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*)
```

--------------------------------

### Destructive Operation Command with Invocation Disabled

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand example protecting destructive operations by setting disable-model-invocation to true. Prevents accidental automated execution of irreversible commands like data deletion.

```markdown
---
description: Delete all test data
disable-model-invocation: true
---
```

--------------------------------

### Complex SlashCommand with All Common Fields

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Comprehensive SlashCommand example demonstrating all common configuration fields: description, argument-hint, allowed-tools with multiple tool specifications, and model selection. Includes embedded commands and structured instructions.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy application to environment
argument-hint: [app-name] [environment] [version]
allowed-tools: Bash(kubectl:*), Bash(helm:*), Read
model: sonnet
---

Deploy $1 to $2 environment using version $3

Pre-deployment checks:
- Verify $2 configuration
- Check cluster status: !`kubectl cluster-info`
- Validate version $3 exists

Proceed with deployment following deployment runbook.
```

--------------------------------

### Frontmatter Field: allowed-tools

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Restricts or specifies which tools a command is permitted to use, enhancing security and clearly documenting required tools.

```APIDOC
## FRONTMATTER FIELD allowed-tools

### Description
Restricts or specifies which tools the command can use.

### Field Name
allowed-tools

### Type
String or Array of strings

### Required
No

### Default
Inherits from conversation permissions

### Constraints
**Formats:**
- Single tool: `allowed-tools: Read`
- Multiple tools (comma-separated): `allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit`
- Multiple tools (array):
```yaml
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash(git:*)
```
**Tool Patterns:**
- Specific tools: `Read`, `Grep`, `Edit`
- Bash with command filter: `Bash(git:*)`, `Bash(npm:*)`, `Bash(docker:*)`
- All tools (not recommended): `"*"`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **allowed-tools** (String or Array of strings) - Optional - Specifies the tools the command is allowed to use.

### Request Example
```yaml
allowed-tools: Read
```
```yaml
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit
```
```yaml
allowed-tools:
  - Read
  - Write
  - Bash(git:*)
```
```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*)
```
```yaml
allowed-tools: "*"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Interactive Workflow Command with Invocation Disabled

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

SlashCommand configured with disable-model-invocation for interactive workflows requiring user input. Ensures setup wizards and guided processes cannot be automated by the model.

```markdown
---
description: Walk through setup wizard
disable-model-invocation: true
---
```

--------------------------------

### Frontmatter Field: description

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Specifies a brief description of the command, which is displayed in the `/help` output for users.

```APIDOC
## FRONTMATTER FIELD description

### Description
Describes what the command does, shown in `/help` output.

### Field Name
description

### Type
String

### Required
No

### Default
First line of command prompt

### Constraints
Max Length: ~60 characters recommended for `/help` display

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **description** (String) - Optional - A brief description of the command's purpose.

### Request Example
```yaml
description: Review code for security issues
```
```yaml
description: Deploy to staging environment
```
```yaml
description: Generate API documentation
```
```

--------------------------------

### Invalid YAML Syntax Error Example

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Demonstrates common YAML syntax error with unclosed quote in frontmatter. Shows incorrect configuration that fails validation and requires fixing quote syntax before command execution.

```yaml
---
description: Missing quote
allowed-tools: Read, Write
model: sonnet
---
```

--------------------------------

### Document Required Tools for Clarity in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML snippet demonstrates how to explicitly document the tools required for a command, such as `Bash(git:*)` and `Read`. Clearly listing tools enhances the command's clarity, indicating its operational dependencies. This practice helps other developers understand the command's capabilities and requirements, ensuring proper tool setup.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*), Read
```

--------------------------------

### Define Specific Tools Explicitly in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example shows how to explicitly list specific tools a command is allowed to use. By detailing tools like `Read`, `Grep`, and `Edit`, this configuration ensures the command operates with a precise set of capabilities. This practice is vital for security, limiting potential actions to only what is necessary.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Edit
```

--------------------------------

### Create Simple Claude Code Markdown Command

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This example demonstrates a basic Claude Code slash command without any YAML frontmatter. It consists solely of the prompt instructions for Claude, making it suitable for straightforward and self-contained tasks. No additional configuration is required for fundamental commands.

```markdown
Review this code for security vulnerabilities including:
- SQL injection
- XSS attacks
- Authentication bypass
- Insecure data handling
```

--------------------------------

### Manual-Only Production Deployment Command

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Complete SlashCommand example for production deployment approval with disable-model-invocation, argument-hint, allowed-tools, and HTML comment documentation. Demonstrates comprehensive configuration for sensitive manual-only operations.

```markdown
---
description: Approve production deployment
argument-hint: [deployment-id]
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Bash(gh:*)
---

<!--
MANUAL APPROVAL REQUIRED
This command requires human judgment and cannot be automated.
-->

Review deployment $1 for production approval:

Deployment details: !`gh api /deployments/$1`

Verify:
- All tests passed
- Security scan clean
- Stakeholder approval
- Rollback plan ready

Type "APPROVED" to confirm deployment.
```

--------------------------------

### Incorrect Tool Specification - Corrected Format

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Shows the corrected format for allowed-tools specification with proper Bash command filter pattern. Demonstrates proper syntax using Bash(git:*) notation for specifying allowed command patterns.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash(git:*)
```

--------------------------------

### disable-model-invocation YAML Configuration - Boolean

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Sets the disable-model-invocation property to true, preventing the SlashCommand tool from programmatically invoking the command. This property is optional and defaults to false when not specified.

```yaml
disable-model-invocation: true
```

--------------------------------

### Incorrect Tool Specification Error Example

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Demonstrates common error of missing command filter in allowed-tools specification. Shows improper Bash tool declaration that requires command pattern specification using the Bash(pattern:*) format.

```yaml
allowed-tools: Bash
```

--------------------------------

### Frontmatter Field: model

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Specifies which Claude AI model executes the command, allowing optimization for speed, capability, and cost.

```APIDOC
## FRONTMATTER FIELD model

### Description
Specifies which Claude model executes the command.

### Field Name
model

### Type
String

### Required
No

### Default
Inherits from conversation

### Constraints
**Values:** `sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **model** (String) - Optional - The Claude model to use for executing the command.

### Request Example
```yaml
model: haiku
```
```yaml
model: sonnet
```
```yaml
model: opus
```
```

--------------------------------

### SlashCommand with Multiple Arguments and Hints

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Complete SlashCommand example demonstrating multiple arguments with descriptive names in argument-hint. The body references each argument using positional parameters ($1, $2, $3) corresponding to the hint order.

```markdown
---
description: Deploy to environment
argument-hint: [app-name] [environment] [version]
---

Deploy $1 to $2 using version $3...
```

--------------------------------

### Invalid Model Name - Corrected Format

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Shows correct Claude model name specification. Valid options are sonnet, opus, or haiku for the model property in SlashCommand configuration.

```yaml
model: sonnet
```

### Automate Claude Command Frontmatter Field Validation Script (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/testing-strategies.md

This Bash script automates the validation of specific fields within a Claude command's YAML frontmatter. It extracts the frontmatter and checks the 'model' field against a predefined list ("sonnet", "opus", "haiku"), verifies the presence of 'allowed-tools', and provides a warning if the 'description' field exceeds a recommended length. This ensures frontmatter data adheres to expected formats and constraints.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# validate-frontmatter.sh

COMMAND_FILE="$1"

# Extract YAML frontmatter
FRONTMATTER=$(sed -n '/^---$/,/^---$/p' "$COMMAND_FILE" | sed '1d;$d')

if [ -z "$FRONTMATTER" ]; then
  echo "No frontmatter to validate"
  exit 0
fi

# Check 'model' field if present
if echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep -q "^model:"; then
  MODEL=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep "^model:" | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d ' ')
  if ! echo "sonnet opus haiku" | grep -qw "$MODEL"; then
    echo "ERROR: Invalid model '$MODEL' (must be sonnet, opus, or haiku)"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "✓ Model field valid: $MODEL"
fi

# Check 'allowed-tools' field format
if echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep -q "^allowed-tools:"; then
  echo "✓ allowed-tools field present"
  # Could add more sophisticated validation here
fi

# Check 'description' length
if echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep -q "^description:"; then
  DESC=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep "^description:" | cut -d: -f2-)
  LENGTH=${#DESC}
  if [ "$LENGTH" -gt 80 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Description length $LENGTH (recommend < 60 chars)"
  else
    echo "✓ Description length acceptable: $LENGTH chars"
  fi
fi

echo "✓ Frontmatter fields valid"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Haiku Model for Simple Tasks in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML snippet demonstrates configuring the `haiku` model for commands that are simple, formulaic, and require fast execution. Using `haiku` optimizes for speed and efficiency, making it suitable for low-complexity tasks or frequent invocations where quick responses are paramount. It's often paired with a brief description.

```yaml
---
description: Format code file
model: haiku
---
```

--------------------------------

### Create Claude Code Markdown Command with YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This snippet illustrates how to add configuration metadata to a Claude Code command using YAML frontmatter. It includes fields like `description`, `allowed-tools`, and `model` to customize command behavior and visibility. The frontmatter block must be at the very top of the Markdown file.

```markdown
---
description: Review code for security issues
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Bash(git:*)
model: sonnet
---

Review this code for security vulnerabilities...
```

--------------------------------

### Define Argument Hint for Claude Code Command (YAML Frontmatter)

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/SKILL.md

This YAML snippet shows how to add an `argument-hint` to a Claude Code command's frontmatter. This hint provides users with expected arguments, improving autocomplete suggestions and overall command usability. It acts as documentation for the command's interface.

```yaml
---
argument-hint: [pr-number] [priority] [assignee]
---
```

--------------------------------

### Extract String Fields from Frontmatter in Bash

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/plugin-settings/references/parsing-techniques.md

Shows bash commands using `grep` and `sed` to extract simple and quoted string values from the frontmatter. It includes logic to remove surrounding quotes for quoted values.

```bash
# Simple value
VALUE=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^field_name:' | sed 's/field_name: *//')

# Quoted value (removes surrounding quotes)
VALUE=$(echo "$FRONTMATTER" | grep '^field_name:' | sed 's/field_name: *//' | sed 's/^"\(.*\)"$/\1/')
```

--------------------------------

### Allow Multiple MCP Tools in Command Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/mcp-integration/references/tool-usage.md

This markdown snippet shows how to specify multiple MCP tools in the `allowed-tools` array within a command's frontmatter. This enables the command to access a group of related tools, such as those for Asana task management, with explicit permissions.

```markdown
---
allowed-tools: [
  "mcp__plugin_asana_asana__asana_create_task",
  "mcp__plugin_asana_asana__asana_search_tasks",
  "mcp__plugin_asana_asana__asana_get_project"
]
---
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Sonnet Model for Balanced Performance in YAML Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

This YAML example shows how to use the `sonnet` model for standard commands requiring a balance of speed and quality. `Sonnet` is the default choice for most common use cases, offering robust performance without the higher cost or latency of more complex models. It's ideal for general code reviews or similar tasks.

```yaml
---
description: Review code changes
model: sonnet
---
```

--------------------------------

### Declare Required Tools with Allowed-Tools

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/examples/plugin-commands.md

Shows how to properly declare and grant permissions for tools used in plugin commands. Without declaring allowed-tools in the frontmatter, commands using Bash or other restricted tools will fail with permission errors.

```markdown
# Missing allowed-tools
!`bash script.sh`  # Will fail without Bash permission

# Correct
---
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---
!`bash ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/script.sh`
```

--------------------------------

### Pre-Allow a Single MCP Tool in Command Frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/mcp-integration/references/tool-usage.md

This markdown snippet demonstrates how to explicitly declare a single MCP tool within a Claude Code command's frontmatter. This `allowed-tools` configuration grants the command permission to use the specified tool, like `mcp__plugin_asana_asana__asana_create_task`.

```markdown
---
description: Create a new Asana task
allowed-tools: [
  "mcp__plugin_asana_asana__asana_create_task"
]
---

# Create Task Command

To create a task:
1. Gather task details from user
2. Use mcp__plugin_asana_asana__asana_create_task with the details
3. Confirm creation to user
```

--------------------------------

### argument-hint YAML Configuration - Descriptive Names

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Illustrates best practice of using descriptive argument names instead of generic placeholders. Clear naming conventions improve user understanding and documentation quality for multi-argument commands.

```yaml
argument-hint: [source-branch] [target-branch] [commit-message]
```

--------------------------------

### argument-hint YAML Configuration - Optional Arguments

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Shows how to document optional arguments alongside required ones using square bracket notation. Optional arguments should be indicated in the command description to clarify their status to users.

```yaml
argument-hint: [file-path] [options]
```

--------------------------------

### argument-hint YAML Configuration - Single Argument

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Defines expected arguments for a SlashCommand with square bracket notation. This example shows a single required argument pattern used for documentation and autocomplete functionality. The argument-hint property is optional and defaults to None.

```yaml
argument-hint: [pr-number]
```

--------------------------------

### argument-hint YAML Configuration - Multiple Arguments

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/plugin-dev/skills/command-development/references/frontmatter-reference.md

Demonstrates multiple required arguments using square bracket notation for each argument. This pattern is used when commands require sequential positional arguments that need to be documented for users and IDE autocomplete systems.

```yaml
argument-hint: [environment] [version]
```
